articles.title: Spirit Airlines Cancels All Flights and Shuts Down Today
articles.summary: Spirit Airlines has shut down completely as of May 2, 2026, cancelling every single flight with immediate effect and beginning a full wind-down of its operations. The collapse leaves thousands of travellers stranded and marks the end of one of America's most recognisable budget carriers. Spirit Airlines had already been through bankruptcy proceedings before this final shutdown.
articles.content:
Spirit Airlines is done. As of today, Saturday May 2, 2026, the airline has cancelled every flight it had scheduled and started winding down all operations. If you had a Spirit Airlines booking, that trip is not happening. The shutdown is immediate and total.

Spirit Airlines was one of the biggest ultra-low-cost carriers in the United States, known for its bright yellow planes and its stripped-back approach to flying where passengers paid extra for almost everything beyond a seat. It had been a go-to option for budget travellers across the country for years. But the airline had been in serious financial trouble for some time, filing for bankruptcy protection in late 2024 as it struggled with debt, rising costs, and fierce competition from other low-cost carriers.

The bankruptcy filing was supposed to be a reset, a chance for Spirit Airlines to restructure and come out the other side as a leaner operation. That plan has now fully collapsed. The wind-down announced today means Spirit Airlines will not be reorganising or relaunching. The airline is gone.

Anyone who had a Spirit Airlines flight booked needs to make alternative arrangements right now. Travellers with credit card travel protection or travel insurance should check their policies immediately. This is also the moment other airlines typically step in with discounted fares to absorb stranded passengers, so checking competitors like Frontier, Southwest, and others is worth doing today. The sudden closure of a major US carrier is a significant moment for the aviation industry and a tough reminder of just how brutal the budget airline business has become.
keyPoints: Spirit Airlines cancelled all flights and shut down operations on May 2, 2026, with immediate effect
The shutdown is a full wind-down, meaning Spirit Airlines will not be reorganising or returning
Spirit Airlines had previously filed for bankruptcy in late 2024 in an attempt to restructure
Thousands of passengers with existing Spirit Airlines bookings are now stranded and need alternative travel plans
The collapse is one of the most significant failures in US budget aviation in recent years

articles.title: Mathematicians Say ChatGPT Helped Crack a Major Number Theory Problem
articles.summary: A team of mathematicians is claiming a significant discovery in number theory after using ChatGPT as a thinking partner throughout their research process. The team says they have found a new way to understand the structure and anatomy of large numbers, which is a foundational area of pure mathematics. The claim is drawing serious attention because it suggests AI tools may now be contributing to genuinely new mathematical knowledge, not just summarising existing work.
articles.content:
Pure mathematics is one of the fields people assumed AI would struggle to crack. It is not about retrieving facts or generating plausible text. It is about rigorous logical proof and genuinely novel ideas. So when a team of mathematicians announced this week that ChatGPT helped them make a significant discovery in number theory, people paid attention.

The researchers described their finding in striking terms. "We have discovered a new way to think about large numbers and their anatomy," they said. Number theory is the branch of mathematics concerned with the properties and relationships of numbers, particularly integers. Understanding the deep structure of large numbers has been a central challenge in the field for centuries, touching everything from cryptography to fundamental questions about prime numbers.

The team did not claim ChatGPT proved anything on its own. Instead, they used it as a collaborative tool, bouncing ideas back and forth and using the model to help them explore and articulate concepts. Think of it less like asking a calculator for an answer and more like having an unusually well-read colleague available at any hour to stress-test your thinking. The researchers say that process led them to a genuinely new framework for how large numbers can be understood.

This is the kind of story that sits right at the edge of what people thought AI could do. ChatGPT and models like it have been criticised heavily for hallucinating mathematical proofs and producing confident-sounding nonsense. But this case suggests that when used carefully by experts who can verify the output, these tools might actually accelerate real discovery rather than just simulate it.

The mathematical community will now need to scrutinise the claim closely. Peer review in pure mathematics is thorough and unforgiving. But if the result holds up, it will be a landmark moment, not just for AI, but for how science and mathematics actually get done. The idea that a general-purpose language model contributed to a genuine breakthrough in one of the oldest and most abstract fields of human knowledge is hard to overstate.
keyPoints: A team of mathematicians claims ChatGPT helped them make a significant discovery in number theory, specifically about the structure of large numbers
The researchers described finding "a new way to think about large numbers and their anatomy"
ChatGPT was used as a collaborative thinking partner rather than as an autonomous proof-generator
The claim has not yet been through full peer review, so independent verification is the next critical step
If confirmed, this would be one of the clearest examples of AI contributing to a genuine breakthrough in pure mathematics
articles.wikiSearchQuery: number theory large numbers mathematics AI

articles.title: Zoë Kravitz and Harry Styles Planning Two Weddings and Babies
articles.summary: Less than a month after Harry Styles proposed with a $500,000 diamond ring, he and Zoë Kravitz are already deep into wedding planning and talking about starting a family. Sources close to the couple tell Page Six they are planning two separate wedding celebrations. The pair got engaged in April 2026 after roughly a year of dating.
articles.content:
Harry Styles and Zoë Kravitz are not wasting any time. Less than a month after Styles slipped a reported $500,000 diamond ring onto Kravitz's finger, the newly engaged couple are already planning not one but two weddings, according to sources who spoke to Page Six.

The details of where and when the celebrations will take place have not been confirmed yet, but the double wedding plan signals just how big this event is going to be. Two weddings typically means two different settings, two different guest lists, and honestly two different vibes. For a couple as high profile as these two, that tracks completely.

And it does not stop at the weddings. Sources say Styles and Kravitz are already talking about having children together. That is a significant step, especially this early in an engagement, and it suggests the two are very much on the same page about building a life together.

Kravitz, known for her roles in films like Batman and the Big Little Lies series, and Styles, the former One Direction member turned solo pop superstar, have kept their relationship relatively private since they were first linked in 2025. But a $500,000 ring and two-wedding plans are about as public a statement as you can make. Fans are already losing their minds over this one.
keyPoints: Harry Styles and Zoë Kravitz got engaged in April 2026 with a $500,000 diamond ring
The couple are planning two separate wedding celebrations
Sources say they are already talking about having babies together
The engagement came roughly a year after the two started dating
Both Kravitz and Styles have kept their relationship mostly private until now

articles.title: Greta Gerwig's Narnia Gets Wide Netflix Theatrical Release in 2027
articles.summary: Netflix is giving Greta Gerwig's Narnia: The Magician's Nephew a wide theatrical release in early 2027, a significant shift from the streaming-first approach Netflix usually takes with its films. Gerwig, who directed the 2023 blockbuster Barbie, is now one of the few filmmakers who can get Netflix to rethink its entire distribution strategy. This is a huge moment for anyone who has been hoping big-budget Netflix films would actually get a proper cinema run.
articles.content:
Greta Gerwig is getting her Narnia film onto the big screen in a serious way. Netflix has confirmed that Narnia: The Magician's Nephew will get a wide theatrical release in early 2027, a real departure from how Netflix typically handles its original movies. For most Netflix films, a brief limited theatrical window is the best you can hope for. Gerwig is getting the full treatment.

For context, Narnia: The Magician's Nephew is an adaptation of the C.S. Lewis novel that serves as the origin story of the magical land of Narnia. It predates the more famous The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe in the book series, explaining how Narnia was created and how the wardrobe got its powers. Netflix acquired the rights to the entire Narnia franchise back in 2018, but progress was slow until Gerwig came on board to direct.

Gerwig's attachment to this project immediately changed the conversation. Her 2023 film Barbie, which she wrote and directed for Warner Bros., became a genuine cultural phenomenon and earned over 1.4 billion dollars at the global box office. It also earned Gerwig significant awards attention, including an Oscar nomination for Best Adapted Screenplay. She is now one of the most commercially proven directors working today, and Netflix clearly wants to maximise what she has made.

The decision to go wide theatrical is a statement. Netflix has been inching toward treating certain films more like traditional studio releases, and Gerwig's Narnia is now the clearest signal yet that Netflix will go full cinema mode when the filmmaker and the material are big enough. Early 2027 gives the film a strong awards season runway if Netflix plays it right.

For fans of the Narnia books and for anyone who loved what Gerwig did with Barbie, this is genuinely exciting news. A wide release means more people will get to experience Narnia: The Magician's Nephew the way it was presumably designed to be seen, on a massive screen with great sound. The combination of Gerwig's visual storytelling instincts and the grand scope of the Narnia universe feels like a very good match.
keyPoints: Netflix is giving Greta Gerwig's Narnia: The Magician's Nephew a wide theatrical release in early 2027
This is a major shift from Netflix's usual streaming-first distribution approach
Gerwig directed the 2023 blockbuster Barbie, which earned over 1.4 billion dollars worldwide
Narnia: The Magician's Nephew tells the origin story of the magical land of Narnia
Netflix acquired the full Narnia franchise rights back in 2018
articles.wikiSearchQuery: The Magician's Nephew Narnia film Netflix

articles.title: GameStop Quietly Built an eBay Stake and Now Wants to Buy It
articles.summary: GameStop is reportedly preparing a formal offer to acquire eBay after secretly building up a stake in the auction platform, according to The Wall Street Journal. This would be a jaw-dropping move for a company that was nearly left for dead just a few years ago. If it goes through, GameStop would transform from a struggling games retailer into the owner of one of the internet's oldest and biggest marketplaces.
articles.content:
GameStop is preparing to make an offer to buy eBay. That sentence would have sounded like a joke five years ago, but The Wall Street Journal reported on Saturday 2 May 2026 that it is very much real. GameStop has reportedly been quietly building a stake in eBay before moving toward a formal acquisition offer.

A little backstory helps here. GameStop was the original meme stock. In early 2021, retail investors on Reddit's WallStreetBets forum drove GameStop's share price to absurd heights, sticking it to hedge funds that had bet against the company. GameStop used that moment to raise cash and has been sitting on a substantial war chest ever since. CEO Ryan Cohen, who made his name selling Chewy and joined GameStop's board in 2021, has been looking for ways to put that money to work.

eBay is one of the original internet giants. Founded in 1995, it was the place people went to buy and sell everything from rare collectibles to second-hand electronics. It has faced increasing competition from Amazon and other platforms over the years but still processes billions of dollars in transactions annually. For GameStop, which already operates in the world of buying and selling used games and collectibles, an eBay acquisition would not be completely out of left field.

The deal is not done. GameStop is reportedly preparing an offer, which means negotiations and regulatory hurdles still lie ahead. But the sheer ambition of the move is striking. GameStop has gone from a company that memes nearly buried to one eyeing a takeover of an internet landmark. Whatever happens next, Ryan Cohen has made sure nobody is sleeping on GameStop anymore.
keyPoints: GameStop is reportedly preparing a formal offer to acquire eBay
GameStop had been quietly building a stake in eBay before moving toward the offer
The Wall Street Journal broke the story on 2 May 2026
GameStop has a large cash reserve built up since the 2021 meme stock surge
No deal has been confirmed and negotiations are still in early stages

articles.title: Michael Hits $300M, Overtakes Elvis as No. 2 Music Biopic Ever
articles.summary: Lionsgate's Michael Jackson biopic Michael has crossed $300 million worldwide, overtaking Warner Bros' Elvis ($288.6M) to become the second highest-grossing music biopic of all time. Only Bohemian Rhapsody, the 2018 Queen biopic that made $911 million globally, sits ahead of it. The film is still in theatres and the gap to Bohemian Rhapsody is enormous, but the milestone cements Michael as a genuine cultural blockbuster.
articles.content:
Lionsgate's Michael has just made history at the box office. As of today, the Michael Jackson biopic has crossed $300 million worldwide, which is enough to knock Elvis off its perch as the number two music biopic ever made. Elvis, the Baz Luhrmann directed Warner Bros film that earned eight Oscar nominations, pulled in $288.6 million during its run. Michael has now cleared that bar.

The only music biopic that has ever done more is Bohemian Rhapsody, the 2018 film about Queen which made a staggering $911 million globally. Notably, Bohemian Rhapsody was also produced by Graham King, who is a producer on Michael as well. That is a remarkable track record for one producer in a single genre.

For context on just how rare this is: music biopics are a crowded field and most of them do not crack $100 million worldwide. Films like Rocket Man, about Elton John, and Whitney Houston's I Wanna Dance with Somebody were considered solid performers without coming close to these numbers. Michael landing at $300 million and counting puts it in a completely different conversation.

The film has not been without controversy. The Jackson family's involvement and questions about how the film handles allegations against Michael Jackson have followed it since production was announced. But audiences have clearly shown up in massive numbers regardless, which tells you something about the enduring power of Jackson's music and cultural legacy. The box office run is not over yet either, so this number will only climb.
keyPoints: Michael has crossed $300 million worldwide at the box office
It has overtaken Elvis ($288.6M) as the number two music biopic of all time
Only Bohemian Rhapsody ($911M) ranks higher in the genre
Both Michael and Bohemian Rhapsody share producer Graham King
The film is still in theatres so the total will keep rising

articles.title: Alex Zanardi, F1 Driver and Paralympic Legend, Dies at 59
articles.summary: Alex Zanardi, the former Formula 1 driver who became a four-time Paralympic cycling champion after losing both legs in a 2001 racing accident, has died at the age of 59. Zanardi was one of sport's most extraordinary comeback stories, winning gold medals at the 2012 and 2016 Paralympic Games after retraining as a hand-cyclist. His death marks the end of a life that inspired millions far beyond motorsport.
articles.content:
Alex Zanardi died on Saturday 2 May 2026 at the age of 59. The Italian athlete had one of the most remarkable careers in the history of sport, moving from Formula 1 circuits to Paralympic podiums in a story that felt almost too extraordinary to be true.

Zanardi raced in Formula 1 during the 1990s, competing for teams including Lotus and Jordan without ever quite finding the results to match his talent. But it was what came after F1 that defined his legacy. In September 2001, while competing in a handcycling race in Germany, Zanardi was involved in a catastrophic accident that resulted in the amputation of both his legs. Most people would never have returned to competitive sport. Zanardi went on to win four Paralympic gold medals.

Using a specially adapted handcycle, Zanardi competed at the 2012 London Paralympics and the 2016 Rio Paralympics, taking gold in multiple events at both Games. He also completed the New York City Marathon and various Ironman triathlons. In 2020 he suffered a severe brain injury after a further handcycling accident in Italy, which led to a long and difficult period of medical care.

For anyone who ever watched him race, Zanardi was proof that the human spirit genuinely has no ceiling. Tributes from the world of motorsport and Paralympic sport poured in on Saturday, with the F1 community among the first to pay their respects. He was 59 years old.
keyPoints: Alex Zanardi died on 2 May 2026 at the age of 59
He raced in Formula 1 in the 1990s before losing both legs in a 2001 accident
Zanardi went on to win four Paralympic gold medals in handcycling in 2012 and 2016
He suffered a severe brain injury in another handcycling accident in 2020
He is remembered as one of sport's greatest comeback stories
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Alex Zanardi racing driver Paralympic cyclist

articles.title: Oscars Ban AI from Acting and Writing Categories
articles.summary: The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences has updated its rules to stop AI-generated performances and writing from qualifying in the acting and writing Oscar categories. The changes also allow actors to be nominated more than once in the same category, which was previously not permitted. It is one of the most significant rule overhauls the Oscars have made in response to the AI era.
articles.content:
The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences just drew a hard line in the sand. As of May 1, 2026, new Oscar rules make it official: AI-generated performances and AI-written scripts will not be eligible in the acting and writing categories. If you want a shot at an Oscar for acting or writing, the work has to come from a human being.

This matters because the conversation about AI in Hollywood has been loud for years, but actual rule changes have been slow to follow. Strikes, debates, and contract negotiations have all circled the question of what counts as a human creative contribution. The Academy just gave that question a concrete answer, at least when it comes to the most prestigious film awards on the planet.

The rule update also includes something that will turn heads among film fans. Actors can now be nominated multiple times in the same category in a single year. Previously, if the Academy voters loved two performances from the same actor, only one could make the ballot. That restriction is now gone, which could make future nomination announcements a lot more interesting.

Think of this as the Oscars trying to future-proof themselves. As AI tools get better at mimicking human performance and generating screenplays, the Academy is making clear that the awards are about celebrating human artistry. Whether these rules are easy to enforce as AI gets more sophisticated is a whole other question, but the intent is now on the record.
keyPoints: The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences has ruled that AI-generated performances and writing are not eligible in the acting and writing Oscar categories
Actors can now be nominated more than once in the same category in a single year, reversing a previous restriction
The rule changes came into effect as of May 2026
The move is one of the most direct responses from a major awards body to the rise of AI in creative industries
Enforcement of AI rules will likely become more complex as AI tools continue to improve

articles.title: Beijing Killed the World's Biggest Digital Rights Conference
articles.summary: RightsCon 2026, the world's largest digital human rights conference, was canceled after China pressured organizers over the inclusion of Taiwanese speakers. The event, run by Access Now, had been scheduled and was scrapped because Beijing objected to Taiwan's representation. It is a stark reminder of how far China's political pressure reaches, even into civil society spaces that have nothing to do with the Chinese government.
articles.content:
RightsCon is the biggest annual gathering for people who care about digital rights. Think journalists, activists, technologists, and human rights lawyers all in one place. Access Now, the nonprofit that runs it, canceled the 2026 edition after intense pressure linked to Beijing's anger over Taiwanese speakers being included on the program.

China's position is simple and unyielding. Taiwan should not be treated as a separate entity from China in any international forum. That pressure has killed sponsorships, pulled companies out of events, and now it has managed to shut down a major human rights conference before it even started.

What makes this particularly jarring is that RightsCon is not a government event. It is a civil society gathering. The people who attend are often fighting against exactly this kind of top-down political silencing. The fact that Beijing's influence reached in and canceled it anyway is a very loud signal about how far that influence now extends.

Access Now has not yet announced whether RightsCon will be rescheduled or restructured. For now, the digital rights community is left without its biggest annual meeting point, and the reason why is written all over the story.
keyPoints: RightsCon, the world's largest digital human rights conference, was canceled due to Chinese government pressure
Beijing was angry that speakers from Taiwan were included in the program
Access Now, the nonprofit behind RightsCon, made the cancellation decision
The cancellation shows China's political pressure now reaches deep into civil society events
No rescheduled date has been announced
articles.wikiSearchQuery: RightsCon digital rights conference Access Now

articles.title: South Africa Pulls AI Policy After Fake Citations Found
articles.summary: South Africa has withdrawn its draft national AI policy after officials discovered the document was AI-generated and cited sources that do not exist. It is a major embarrassment for the government and a cautionary tale about using AI to write official policy. The story is already going viral as a perfect example of what happens when no one checks the machine's work.
articles.content:
South Africa's government has pulled its draft national AI policy from the table after a pretty stunning discovery: the whole thing was generated by AI, and the sources it cited were completely made up. Not outdated, not misquoted. Just fictional. The policy was supposed to chart a course for how South Africa would handle artificial intelligence as a technology and a societal force. Instead, it became a case study in AI going very wrong.

This kind of error has a name. It is called hallucination, which is what happens when an AI language model generates plausible-sounding but entirely false information, including fake book titles, fake journal articles, and fake expert names. The problem is well known in AI circles, but it is alarming to see it surface in a government document meant to guide national policy.

The withdrawal is a big deal because it raises a serious question: how many other official documents, reports, or policies have been quietly drafted with AI help and never properly fact-checked? South Africa is not alone in exploring AI tools for government work. But this incident puts every similar effort on notice. If you do not verify the output, you could be building policy on a foundation of nothing.

For anyone watching the global conversation around AI governance, this is a deeply ironic moment. A country trying to write rules about AI got burned by AI doing exactly what critics have been warning about. The story is equal parts cautionary tale and dark comedy, and it is the kind of real-world example that will likely show up in classrooms, policy conferences, and tech debates for years to come.
keyPoints: South Africa withdrew its draft national AI policy after discovering it was AI-generated
The policy cited sources that do not exist, a problem known as AI hallucination
No one caught the errors before the document became public, raising accountability concerns
The incident is a real-world warning about using AI to draft official government documents without proper fact-checking

articles.title: Lizzo Announces Album Bitch and Declares It Bitch Summer
articles.summary: Lizzo has officially announced her new album Bitch and dropped the lead single also called Bitch, declaring that Bitch Summer is here. The announcement caps off a slow-burn comeback that began last year after Lizzo spent years navigating serious lawsuit battles. She is making it very clear the Special era is over and a new chapter has started.
articles.content:
Lizzo is back and she is not easing in gently. On May 1, 2026, the singer announced her new album Bitch, shared the lead single Bitch, and told the world in no uncertain terms that it is officially Bitch Summer. If the name did not make the energy clear enough, the rollout absolutely does.

This comeback has actually been building for a while. Lizzo started her return last year with the single Love In Real Life, which came with a post that said a clear goodbye to her previous Special era. Special was the 2022 album that won her a Grammy for Album of the Year, so closing that chapter was a big deal. Since then she has been teasing new material and the wait has genuinely felt long to fans who were hoping for a full project.

The lawsuit backdrop matters here too. Lizzo spent a significant stretch dealing with highly publicised legal battles involving former dancers, which put a serious dent in her public profile. Those cases were bruising. Coming back with an album called Bitch is not a subtle move. It reads like someone who went through the fire and came out deciding to own every part of her story rather than soften it.

The single Bitch gives a strong first impression of where the album is headed. If the rest of Bitch matches the tone and confidence of this rollout, Lizzo has a real shot at flipping the narrative entirely. Bitch Summer might just be a real thing.
keyPoints: Lizzo announced her new album Bitch and released the lead single also titled Bitch
She declared this season Bitch Summer as part of the rollout
The comeback began last year with the single Love In Real Life, which closed out her Special era
The album follows years of high-profile lawsuit battles that damaged her public image
Special won Lizzo a Grammy for Album of the Year in 2023, making this follow-up a major moment

articles.title: Meta Buys Assured Robot Intelligence to Power Humanoid AI
articles.summary: Meta has acquired Assured Robot Intelligence, a humanoid robotics startup, to strengthen the AI models that will run its robots. The deal signals that Meta is getting serious about competing in the humanoid robot space alongside companies like Figure and Tesla. No financial terms were disclosed, but the acquisition gives Meta a team with deep expertise in making AI work reliably inside physical machines.
articles.content:
Meta just made its most concrete move yet into the world of physical AI. The company announced it has bought Assured Robot Intelligence, a startup focused on building the AI brains that let humanoid robots operate safely and dependably in the real world. This is not a research experiment. Meta is using Assured Robot Intelligence to directly beef up the AI models powering its robotics ambitions.

Humanoid robots are having a genuine moment right now. Figure, 1X, and Tesla's Optimus are all racing to put two-legged robots into warehouses and homes. Meta has been watching from the sidelines for a while, investing in AI research but not yet showing a robot of its own. Buying Assured Robot Intelligence is Meta saying it wants a seat at that table.

What Assured Robot Intelligence brings to Meta is expertise in making AI reliable enough for a robot body. That sounds simple but it is genuinely hard. A language model can hallucinate and you fix it with a correction. A robot that makes the wrong decision can knock something over or hurt someone. The safety and reliability layer is what Assured Robot Intelligence was built to solve.

Meta has not shown a humanoid robot yet and has not given a timeline for when it might. But between this acquisition and Meta's broader AI infrastructure push, the pieces are starting to come together. The humanoid robot race just got a much bigger player paying close attention.
keyPoints: Meta acquired humanoid robotics startup Assured Robot Intelligence
The deal is aimed at improving AI models that will run Meta's robots
Meta is entering a humanoid robot race already featuring Figure, 1X, and Tesla's Optimus
Assured Robot Intelligence specializes in making AI reliable enough for physical robot operation
No financial terms for the acquisition were disclosed

articles.title: Zayn Malik Cancels US Tour After Hospitalisation
articles.summary: Zayn Malik has cancelled his entire US tour and cut several UK dates after being hospitalised, throwing his biggest-ever solo tour into chaos. Malik told fans he hopes to return 'better and stronger than ever before' as he recovers. The tour was set to begin later this month and would have been the largest headline run of his solo career.
articles.content:
Zayn Malik was supposed to be kicking off the biggest tour of his solo career later this month. Instead, the former One Direction member has announced he is cancelling all US tour dates and cutting a number of UK shows after being hospitalised. Malik shared the news directly with fans, telling them he hopes to come back 'better and stronger than ever before' once he has recovered.

Zayn Malik has not revealed the specific nature of his hospitalisation, and details remain limited. What is clear is that the scale of the cancellations is significant. This was meant to be his most ambitious live run as a solo artist, so losing the US leg entirely is a major blow to both Malik and his fans who had been waiting years to see him perform on this scale.

Malik has had a complicated relationship with live performance since leaving One Direction in 2015. He famously pulled out of several performances over the years citing anxiety, so fans have always been aware that touring does not come easily to him. This makes the fact that he had committed to such a large run all the more meaningful, and the news of its cancellation all the more gutting for people who had bought tickets.

No rescheduled dates have been confirmed yet. If you have tickets for any of the cancelled shows, check with your ticket provider about refunds. Malik's message to fans was warm and optimistic, which at least suggests he is hoping to make this right when his health allows.
keyPoints: Zayn Malik has cancelled his full US tour after being hospitalised
Several UK dates have also been cut from the schedule
This was set to be his biggest solo tour ever
Malik told fans he hopes to return stronger once he has recovered
No rescheduled dates have been announced yet

articles.title: Eric Trump's Crypto Company Scrubs His Name After Total Meltdown
articles.summary: A crypto company publicly associated with Eric Trump quietly removed his name from its leadership page following major legal and financial turmoil. The move suggests the company is trying to distance itself from Trump rather than the other way around, which is a notable reversal. The situation is being described as a total disaster.
articles.content:
Things have gone seriously wrong for the crypto company that had been trading on Eric Trump's name, and now the company wants nothing to do with him. According to Futurism, the firm quietly scrubbed Eric Trump's name from its public leadership materials after getting hit with major legal and financial trouble. No announcement, no explanation. His name just disappeared.

This kind of quiet deletion is a red flag in any industry, but in crypto it is a particularly familiar move. When things go sideways, projects often try to quietly distance themselves from the faces they used to attract attention and investment in the first place. The fact that Eric Trump's name was prominent enough to be worth featuring and then suddenly too toxic to keep is a telling detail about how fast things deteriorated.

Eric Trump is the son of Donald Trump and has been publicly involved in various business ventures alongside his family's political and commercial brand. Crypto has been a space where the Trump family name has come up repeatedly in recent years, with multiple projects and endorsements circling the ecosystem. That connection has sometimes drawn scrutiny about whether star power was being used to lend credibility to projects that did not necessarily deserve it.

What the specific legal and financial issues are that triggered this particular scrubbing has not been fully detailed publicly, but the act of removing someone's name from a company's official materials without any public statement is itself a story. It signals internal panic. Investors and observers in the crypto space are now watching closely to see what comes next from this project and whether the full picture of what went wrong will eventually surface.
keyPoints: A crypto company quietly deleted Eric Trump's name from its public leadership page
The removal followed serious legal and financial trouble at the company
No public explanation was given for scrubbing his name
The move suggests the company was distancing itself from Eric Trump rather than vice versa
The Trump family name has been linked to multiple crypto projects in recent years

articles.title: Post Malone Delays Big Ass Stadium Tour: 'We Ain't Ready Just Yet'
articles.summary: Post Malone has cancelled the opening dates of his Big Ass Stadium tour with Jelly Roll, saying he needs more time to finish new music he promised fans. Post Malone shared the news directly with fans, explaining that he refused to hit the stage without delivering on that promise. No new start date has been confirmed yet.
articles.content:
Post Malone has pulled the brakes on the start of his Big Ass Stadium tour with Jelly Roll. The announcement came on Saturday 2 May 2026, with Post Malone addressing fans directly and keeping it completely honest about why the shows are not happening as planned.

"The truth is, I promised y'all beautiful people new music, and I don't have the time to finish it before tour starts," Post Malone said. He followed that up with the summary that said it all: "We ain't ready for tour just yet." It is a rare move in the music industry, where artists usually push through regardless, but Post Malone has clearly decided that showing up without the goods is not an option.

The Big Ass Stadium tour was set to be a massive joint run featuring Post Malone and Jelly Roll, two of the biggest names in the current crossover space between hip-hop, country, and pop. Jelly Roll has had a meteoric rise over the past couple of years, going from an underground favourite to a mainstream star with a loyal and passionate fanbase. Pairing him with Post Malone for a stadium run was the kind of bill that sells out quickly, which makes the delay sting even more for ticketholders.

The postponement is frustrating for fans who already have tickets and travel booked, but Post Malone's reasoning is at least refreshingly straightforward. He is not hiding behind vague "scheduling conflicts" or blaming a mystery illness. He wants the music to be done before he asks people to show up and pay for a show built around it.

No rescheduled dates have been announced yet. Both Post Malone and Jelly Roll have huge fanbases who will be watching closely for updates. The optimistic read here is that when this tour eventually does happen, it will come with a proper new Post Malone project to back it up, which could make it an even bigger event than originally planned.
keyPoints: Post Malone has cancelled the opening dates of the Big Ass Stadium tour with Jelly Roll
He said he promised fans new music and does not have time to finish it before tour starts
Post Malone's direct quote was 'We ain't ready for tour just yet'
No rescheduled dates have been announced
Jelly Roll is co-headlining the tour

articles.title: Bella Ramsey, Michael Sheen Join Celebrity Traitors Season 2
articles.summary: The Celebrity Traitors is returning to BBC for a second season with a seriously stacked cast including Bella Ramsey, Michael Sheen, and Richard E. Grant. The show is one of the buzziest reality formats on British TV right now, mixing celebrity drama with a game of deception and betrayal. Having three heavy hitters like Ramsey, Sheen, and Grant in the same room is a very good sign for what is coming.
articles.content:
The Celebrity Traitors is back, and the BBC has gone all out on the casting. Bella Ramsey, Michael Sheen, and Richard E. Grant are among the stars signed up for the second season, and honestly that lineup alone is enough to get excited about.

If you have never watched The Traitors, here is the quick version. A group of players are split into two secret groups: the Traitors, who try to eliminate others without being caught, and the Faithfuls, who try to figure out who the Traitors are. The celebrity version keeps all that tension but layers in the fact that these are famous faces trying to lie to each other on national television, which makes it even more fun to watch.

Bella Ramsey is probably best known right now for playing Ellie in HBO's The Last of Us, so watching her try to deceive and outmanoeuvre fellow celebrities is a very different energy. Michael Sheen is one of the most versatile actors in British film and TV, and Richard E. Grant has been a beloved screen presence for decades going back to his breakout in Withnail and I. Putting all three of them in a game built entirely around trust and lies feels almost unfair in the best possible way.

Season one of The Celebrity Traitors was a hit for the BBC and helped cement the Traitors format as one of the most talked about reality shows in the UK. Season two clearly has the momentum to go even bigger. Keep an eye on the premiere date because this one is going to be all over your timeline the moment it drops.
keyPoints: The Celebrity Traitors Season 2 is confirmed for BBC with a major cast
Bella Ramsey, Michael Sheen, and Richard E. Grant are all joining the show
The Traitors format pits Faithfuls against secret Traitors in a game of deception
Season one was a BBC hit, making season two one of the most anticipated reality returns
Bella Ramsey is best known for playing Ellie in The Last of Us on HBO
articles.wikiSearchQuery: The Celebrity Traitors BBC television series

articles.title: Apple Raises Mac Mini Starting Price to $799
articles.summary: Apple has raised the Mac Mini's starting price to $799, up from $599, a $200 jump that will sting budget-conscious buyers. The Mac Mini has long been Apple's most affordable desktop entry point, so this price increase is a meaningful shift for anyone hoping to get into the Apple ecosystem cheaply. Apple has not officially explained the reason for the increase.
articles.content:
The Mac Mini just got a lot more expensive. Apple quietly raised the starting price of the Mac Mini to $799, which is a $200 jump from its previous $599 entry price. For a machine that was specifically priced to be the affordable way into Apple's desktop lineup, that is a significant move.

The Mac Mini has always had a specific role in Apple's world. It is the machine you buy when you want macOS but already have a monitor, keyboard, and mouse. Students, creative professionals on a budget, and people switching from Windows have all relied on the Mac Mini as the sensible, low-cost option. At $799 it is still cheaper than an iMac, but the gap between the Mac Mini and a full system just got a lot smaller.

Apple has not given a public explanation for the price increase. Possible reasons include component cost changes, tariff pressure on imported goods, or Apple simply deciding the Mac Mini can carry a higher margin. None of those reasons make the news easier to swallow for buyers who were counting on the old price.

If you were planning to buy a Mac Mini at the $599 price, that option is gone. The new $799 starting price is what Apple is selling right now. It is worth watching whether this price change affects Mac Mini sales or pushes budget buyers toward refurbished models or competitors.
keyPoints: Apple has raised the Mac Mini starting price from $599 to $799
That is a $200 increase on Apple's most affordable desktop
Apple gave no official explanation for the price hike
The Mac Mini remains cheaper than an iMac but the gap has narrowed
Budget buyers may now look at refurbished Mac Minis or alternative options

articles.title: Diego Luna Joins Tangled Remake in a Brand New Role
articles.summary: Diego Luna, best known as Cassian Andor in the Star Wars series Andor, has joined the cast of Disney's live-action Tangled remake in a character created specifically for the new film. The role does not exist in the original 2010 animated movie, which means Disney is expanding the story rather than just recreating it. No details about the character have been revealed yet.
articles.content:
Disney's live-action Tangled remake just got a very interesting new addition. Diego Luna, the Mexican actor who played rebel spy Cassian Andor in the acclaimed Star Wars series Andor on Disney Plus, has reportedly joined the cast in a brand new role built from scratch for this version of the film.

Tangled, for anyone who missed it the first time around, is the 2010 Disney animated film that retells the story of Rapunzel, the girl with magical impossibly long hair locked in a tower. It was a massive hit and introduced the song "I See the Light," which became one of the most recognisable Disney ballads of its era. A live-action remake has been in the works for a while, following Disney's long-running strategy of revisiting its animated classics with real actors.

What makes Luna's casting especially interesting is that the character was created just for the live-action version. That tells us Disney is not simply doing a shot-for-shot remake. They are building out the world, adding new layers and presumably new plot threads. Luna has serious dramatic range, as Andor proved, and a completely original character gives him room to do something genuinely unexpected.

No details about who he is playing have been shared yet, which means the speculation is already running hot. Is he a villain? An ally? Someone connected to Rapunzel's backstory? We do not know yet. But Diego Luna showing up in a Disney princess film as a mystery character created just for this movie is exactly the kind of casting news that makes a project feel like it has genuine ambition behind it.
keyPoints: Diego Luna has joined the cast of Disney's live-action Tangled remake
His character is entirely new and does not appear in the original 2010 animated film
Luna is best known for playing Cassian Andor in the Star Wars series Andor
No details about the new character have been revealed yet
Disney adding original characters suggests this remake will expand the story rather than just retell it

articles.title: Rio Fashion Week Returns in 2026 With a Hot New Era
articles.summary: Rio Fashion Week made its highly anticipated 2026 comeback and it did not show up quietly. The relaunch signals a major moment for Brazilian fashion on the global stage. Designers came ready, the energy was high, and the city reminded everyone why it deserves a seat at the big table.
articles.content:
Rio Fashion Week is back, and it came in swinging. After a period away from the spotlight, the 2026 relaunch brought Brazilian fashion roaring back into the conversation with a lineup that felt fresh, bold, and unapologetically local. Rio is not trying to be Paris or Milan. It is doing its own thing, and right now that is exactly what makes it exciting.

The shows leaned into everything that makes Brazilian design distinct. Vivid colour, body-conscious silhouettes, and a sense of joy that a lot of global fashion has been missing lately. There was a real feeling that designers had stored up ideas during the downtime and were finally letting them all out at once. The result was a week that felt more like a celebration than a trade event.

Brazilian fashion has long punched below its weight internationally, partly because Rio Fashion Week itself had an inconsistent run. Relaunching in 2026 with this kind of energy is a statement of intent. The industry is watching to see whether Rio can build on this momentum and carve out a permanent place on the international fashion calendar.

If this relaunch is any indication, the answer is yes. Rio Fashion Week 2026 was not just a comeback. It was a reintroduction. And the rest of the fashion world would do well to pay attention.
keyPoints: Rio Fashion Week made its 2026 comeback after time away from the global spotlight
Brazilian designers brought bold colour, strong silhouettes, and a distinct local identity
The relaunch positions Rio as a serious player on the international fashion calendar
The energy of the week felt more like a cultural celebration than a standard industry event

articles.title: Gremlins Assembly Cut From 1983 Screened Publicly for the First Time
articles.summary: A secret screening event just showed an assembly cut of the 1984 classic Gremlins that had not been seen publicly since 1983, giving a room full of horror filmmakers a look at the movie in a form no audience had ever watched before. The event drew a remarkable crowd including Adam B. Stein and Zach Lipovsky, who are writing Gremlins 3, as well as directors Drew Hancock, Rob Savage, and Brian Duffield. An assembly cut is the very first rough edit of a film before any polishing or trimming has been done, meaning this version would be noticeably different from the Gremlins audiences know.
articles.content:
Someone just showed a room full of horror directors something almost no one alive has seen. A secret screening event revealed the assembly cut of Gremlins, the beloved 1984 Joe Dante horror comedy, for the first time since 1983. That means this rough, unpolished early edit of the film sat unseen for over four decades before being brought out for this event.

For anyone who needs a quick refresher, Gremlins is the Steven Spielberg produced film about a boy who receives a mysterious creature called a Mogwai as a pet, accidentally breaks the rules for caring for it, and unleashes chaos in the form of mischievous monsters called Gremlins. It is considered a landmark in 1980s genre filmmaking and helped lead to the creation of the PG-13 rating in the US because it was too intense for kids but not quite R-rated material.

The crowd at this screening reads like a fantasy dinner party for horror fans. Adam B. Stein and Zach Lipovsky, the filmmakers behind Final Destination: Bloodlines who are also writing Gremlins 3, were there. So were Drew Hancock, who directed the 2025 sci-fi thriller Companion, Rob Savage who made The Boogeyman, and Brian Duffield who wrote and directed Whalefall. These are not casual fans. These are working filmmakers who study craft for a living, and they all showed up for this.

An assembly cut is significant because it shows the raw material before a director and editor have shaped it into the film you know. Scenes that were cut entirely might appear. The pacing is usually much slower. Dialogue and sound are often rough. Watching the Gremlins assembly cut would be like reading an early draft of a favourite novel and seeing what almost was.

The timing is fascinating given that Gremlins 3 is actively in development with Stein and Lipovsky writing the script. Watching the original film in its rawest form right as a new chapter is being crafted feels like a deliberate and inspired choice. Whether this screening was connected to the development of Gremlins 3 or simply a celebration of the original, it has clearly got the horror world buzzing. Do not be surprised if this becomes a talking point at every genre festival for the rest of the year.
keyPoints: The Gremlins assembly cut was shown publicly for the first time since 1983
An assembly cut is the rough first edit of a film before any polishing
The screening drew top horror directors including the writers of Gremlins 3
Adam B. Stein and Zach Lipovsky, who are writing Gremlins 3, attended the event
The original Gremlins was produced by Steven Spielberg and released in 1984
